issues: white triangular chain element has a stark flat quality that reads as slightly pasted rather than native pixel art
item is small relative to scene — spottability without the guide arrow is reduced
white top portion could briefly read as a UI indicator or directional arrow at first glance
reasoning: The amulet is placed on the dark reddish dungeon floor tiles where both the bright white chain-clasp and the saturated green gem provide reasonable luminance and chroma contrast. The silhouette (inverted-triangle top + teardrop gem) is distinct from background tiles and the nearby slime entity. However, the white geometric top looks somewhat flat and pasted against the textured dark tile surface — it lacks the weathered pixel-art shading that would make it feel native. The green gem reads clearly as a pickup item and is the strongest element. Overall the asset is locatable with moderate effort but not instantly spottable in 0.5 s without the red-arrow hint; integration quality is acceptable but below the clean Echoes aesthetic standard.
